metadata.nature_of_this_file:
Bootstrap pointer prepared by Guide instance 7 for the Lead Architect (LA) to attach when spawning a fresh EA chat for the Stage 3 EA. This file does NOT introduce new procedural instructions -- it points at the locked Stage 3 XML, the Stage 2 audit chain (5 EA mid-stage handoff XMLs at ea_outputs/, 9 Guide-#5 disposition files, 16 Guide-#6 disposition files at temp_for_responses/, 4 EA comprehension gates iter-1/iter-2, 4 EA initialization pointers EA-3/4/5/6, the Guide-#6 closing-handoff verdict file, and the GUIDE_HANDOFF_LATEST.xml brief from instance 6 to 7), and the binding ack chain. All actual procedural authority lives in those source files; this pointer enumerates IDs + status flags + source citations so the EA's iteration-1 comprehension gate can restate verbatim from the cited source files.
Per Guide-#6 closing-handoff guidance (file `2.6-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_6_STAGE_2_CLOSED_HANDOFF_RECOMMENDATIONS.xml`) and per the handoff brief at `GUIDE_HANDOFF_LATEST.xml` §first_action_for_successor: - Stage 3 EA chain re-numbers from "instance 1" (each stage's EA chain restarts). - The 47 unique-ID binding acks (33 v4-era + 4 g6-ea5 + 10 g6-ea6, all SATISFIED or AUDIT-HISTORY) carry forward as the audit-chain inheritance for Stage 3. New g7-ea1 acks (FORWARD-ACTIVE) are introduced at the bottom of `ack_chain_carried_forward` to bind Stage 3 specifics. - Numerical reconciliation with handoff brief (post-LA-directive disk verification at 2026-04-27 by Guide-#7 via grep-driven enumeration of every `<ack id=` and `<ack n=` element across docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/ and docs/platform_separation/ea_outputs/): the handoff brief's "41 v4-era + 4 g6-ea5 + 10 g6-ea6 = 55" total decomposes against disk reality as follows: - **5 REAL acks** missed by the EA-6 init pointer's "33 v4-era" canonical count: * g4-iter2-1 (verbatim source: 2.5-EA_ACKNOWLEDGMENTS_PROCEED_TO_ITEM_2.4.xml:13) * g4-iter2-2 (verbatim source: 2.5-EA_ACKNOWLEDGMENTS_PROCEED_TO_ITEM_2.4.xml:14) * g4-iter2-3 (verbatim source: 2.5-EA_ACKNOWLEDGMENTS_PROCEED_TO_ITEM_2.4.xml:15) * f1 (verbatim source: ea_outputs/EA_HANDOFF_STAGE2_MIDSTAGE_v4.xml:343 -- defined with `` as a SEPARATE ID alongside g5-5; Guide-#5 v4 quality review D2 explicitly counts it as separate per line 72: "1 f1 (treated as separate ack)". EA-6 init pointer collapsed f1 = g5-5 conceptually, but on disk f1 has its own ack-element identifier) * dn15 (verbatim source: ea_outputs/EA_HANDOFF_STAGE2_MIDSTAGE_v5.xml:271 -- defined with `Docs-Native handoff cadence (even slightly tight budget at clean breakpoint = handoff). FORWARD-ACTIVE.`. NAMESPACE-COLLISION NOTE: the `dn` prefix is also used by GUIDE_HANDOFF_LATEST.xml `do_not_list` for prohibitions dn1-dn24; these are different namespaces despite the prefix collision. Disk position confirms `dn15` here is a binding ack, not a prohibition ID cross-reference.) - **3 PHANTOM acks** that the handoff brief inflated from a v4-handoff authoring miscount that Guide-#5's v4 quality review explicitly REJECTED: * i1-5 (does not exist on disk; only "i1-1..i1-7" group-reference appears in ea_outputs/EA_HANDOFF_STAGE2_MIDSTAGE_v4.xml:334 + v5.xml:261 as a v4-handoff authoring artifact) * i1-6 (same; phantom) * i1-7 (same; phantom) Authoritative disk-verified rejection at 2.5-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_5_V4_QUALITY_REVIEW_DEFECTS_FOUND.xml:67 verbatim: "4 plus_iteration_1 (i1-1, i1-2, i1-3, i1-4) -- v4 says 'i1-1..i1-7' which is WRONG (only 4 exist)" The canonical iter-1 ack source is 2.4-RESPONSE-EA_COMPREHENSION_GATE_ITERATION_2_STAGE2.xml lines 57-60 -- only 4 i1-N elements are defined (`` through ``), no i1-5/6/7 anywhere on disk.
DISPOSITION (per LA's "do not hallucinate" directive at 2026-04-27): the 5 REAL missed acks are added below in the `preserved_from_v4_era` block as new ack_groups (g4-iter2 + f1 + dn15). The 3 PHANTOM acks are NOT added; instead, they are documented in the `phantom_acks_documented_not_real` block below the v4-era acks.
POST-CORRECTION CANONICAL COUNT (LA-confirmed at 2026-04-27 after Guide-#7 recommendation; mature-not-minimal disposition): **52 unique-ID predecessor acks** = 38 v4-era (33 EA-6-init-pointer-canonical + 5 real missed) + 4 g6-ea5 + 10 g6-ea6. Plus 9 g7-ea1 acks introduced in this Guide-#7 init pointer for Stage 3 specifics = **61 total binding acks** for Stage 3 EA's iter-1 gate restatement.
The handoff brief's "55" was inflated by the rejected v4-handoff "i1-1..i1-7" authoring miscount (phantom i1-5/6/7). Per LA's "do not hallucinate" + "we resolve and address our mistakes according to best practice" directives at 2026-04-27, the phantom 3 are NOT bound; they are documented in the `phantom_acks_documented_not_real` block below for audit-chain transparency. This mirrors the Stage 2 procedural precedent at 2.10.1 (Guide-#6 dn19 OVERRIDE produced a forward-correction commit when EA-6's 2.10 footnotes drifted from disposition-file truth) and the Guide-#5 v4 quality review precedent (forward-correction in the next init pointer; v4 handoff stays as-is per no-amend doctrine dn9/dn23).
The handoff brief at GUIDE_HANDOFF_LATEST.xml is NOT amended (per dn9/dn23 no-amend doctrine on committed audit artifacts); this init pointer is the canonical forward-corrected statement. Stage 3.10 STATUS append (per g7-ea1_n9) MUST include a footnote documenting the ack-count correction (mirrors the v4 D2 footnote + v5 C1-C4 footnote pattern at Stage 2.10).

ea_initiation.identity: You are Stage 3 Executor Agent (EA) instance 1, the FIRST EA of the Stage 3 chain. Stage 3 is MEDIUM risk per Master Plan §6 (vs HIGH for Stage 2 + Stage 4). The comprehension-gate block at the top of `04_STAGE3_COPY_TOOLS.xml` is mandatory: present a structured comprehension summary AND restate every binding acknowledgment verbatim, then STOP and WAIT for Guide instance 7 review before any edits, branches, file copies, or commands. The LA's "mature not minimal" mandate (p3 + p16 in the GUIDE_HANDOFF_LATEST la_profile) applies throughout: thoroughness wins over speed. EA-6's procedural track record across Stage 2 (4 disposition cycles each surfaced cleanly via STOP-and-report-with-options framing, independent disk verification on every EA report, paraphrase-error-self-correction via in-scope follow-on commits) is the discipline standard you inherit.

context_summary_what_just_happened.stage_2_substantive_outcome:
Stage 2 closed at all 4 axes (structural + narrative + audit-chain + invariant) per the final Guide-#6 verdict file `docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/2.6-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_6_STAGE_2_CLOSED_HANDOFF_RECOMMENDATIONS.xml`.
Substantive work folded into single commit 3d1ac35 (parent f863a22; 69 files / +12955 -74): - tools/_vikunja_client.py (NEW): chokepoint with `_require_project_id` rejection of None/bool/non-int/<=0 (per f1 fix authorized via Guide-#5 disposition Option A; extended at v4 corrections cycle with 2 NET-ADD tests). HTTP delegation intentionally stubbed; Stage 5+ full-fold deferred per ack g5-4 ext. - tools/_project_context.py (NEW): 5-tier resolution order (cli > env > walk-up registry > walk-up .git > fallback); rejects bool project_id (per f1); uses PyYAML; explicit error on missing registry. ProjectContext dataclass (root + vikunja_project_id + name). - tools/autonomy_budget/projects/blarai.yaml (NEW): per-project allowlist overlay (renamed schema; no Python __init__.py since projects/ is a YAML data root). - 13 Python entrypoints refactored to thread ProjectContext + accept --project-root / --project-id flags (alias --blarai-root preserved per A11 anomaly). Files include the 6 D2-completion targets that received the Guide-#6 Option A HOIST disposition for cfg resolution above the LiveVikunjaClient constructor. - 6 PS1 helpers (wake_launcher.ps1, agents-cadence-monitor.ps1, la_merge_approve.ps1, escalation_watchdog.ps1, toast_watchdog.ps1, test_async_post_gate.ps1) gained -BlarAIRoot parameter with BLARAI_ROOT env-fallback (G-1 + G-4 closure per R12). Verified by AST-driven probe at scripts/verify_25_v2.ps1 (20/20 PASS). - tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py (legacy): D2-completion via Option A HOIST in 3 divergent files (daily_digest, weekly_summary, run_live) + AFTER pattern `base_url=cfg["vikunja"]["base_url"]` uniformly across all 6 callers (3 client + 3 live; assignee preserved per r1). D3 closure via NEW public update_task method on LiveVikunjaClient (lines ~105-115). g5-4 SATISFIED at Item 2.7. - tools/vikunja_mcp/server.py: D4 DIVERGE-APPROVED retention (chokepoint-function-as- integration via 7 _require_project_id sites at handler entry; class-method routing deferred to Stage 5+ per Guide-#6 inter-deliverable review). Docstring fix at lines 22 + 24-25 + 53 ("all 8" -> "all 7"; phantom 8th reference removed) per g6-ea6_n9 dn19 authorization. Optional project_summary clarification note added. - 4 V matrix tests at tools/tests/ (V4 = 12 cases CLI flag exposure; V5 = 1 case PS1 -BlarAIRoot via verify_25_v2 probe; V6 = 50 cases MCP project_id rejection with self-discovering audit test using inspect.getsource; V7 = 6 cases cross-project structural byte-identity). pytest tools/ baseline 282 -> 351 (NET-ADD +69; zero regression). - pyproject.toml in diff (testpaths += tools/tests/; per i1-3).
Stage 2 close commit chain: - f863a22 (v5 mid-stage handoff XML, EA-5 emitted at item 2.6 -> 2.10 transition) - 3d1ac35 (single Stage-2 substantive commit, EA-6) - 89213ee (Stage 2 close STATUS append; +59/-5 STATUS.md changes; Guide-#6 Item-2.9 verdict XML archived per artifact-management guidance; 12-item Stage 2 Closure Footnotes section + Anomaly A11 + Stage Ledger PENDING -> COMPLETE) - 947cbd5 (Stage 2 closure footnote narrative corrections per Item 2.10.1; 5 N-corrections authorized via g6-ea6_n10 per Guide-#6 dn19 OVERRIDE of EA-6's rule-5b(a) framing) - 0d61581 (LA's commit of GUIDE_HANDOFF_LATEST.xml; instance 6 to 7 handoff brief)
context_summary_what_just_happened.ea_6_paraphrase_error_pattern_observed: Notable observation from Guide-#6 closing handoff (carried forward as w11 in handoff brief): EA-6's recurring LOW-MED severity narrative errors at v5 count_delta misattribution + file 13 "8 vs 7" docstring drift + 2.10 footnotes 5-error inventory + closing-handoff chat narrative V matrix file misnaming. Each prior cycle was corrected via in-scope follow-on commits (v5 verification correction C1; docstring fix at 3d1ac35; 2.10.1 narrative corrections at 947cbd5; closing-handoff chat narrative is informational only / not committed). The discipline cuts both ways: EA-6's structural axes (commit mechanics, append-only discipline, invariant preservation) were exemplary; the paraphrase-narrative axis introduced errors that disk-verification caught. Stage 3 EA inherits this discipline reminder: independent disk verification on every claim; verbatim restatement from source files; surface paraphrase errors via in-scope follow-on commits if introduced.

ack_chain_carried_forward.intro:
All 47 unique-ID binding acks (33 v4-era + 4 g6-ea5 + 10 g6-ea6) carry forward as audit chain inheritance. Each is restated below with status flag + source_file attribute. Verbatim text is included inline for the 14 acks where Guide-#7 has direct disposition file source on hand (g6-ea5_n1-n4 + g6-ea6_n1-n10); the 33 v4-era acks are restated in canonical compressed form per the established EA-6 init pointer pattern (status flags + source citations) -- the Stage 3 EA's iteration-1 comprehension gate restates each verbatim from the cited source files. NEW g7-ea1 acks bound at the end are FORWARD-ACTIVE for Stage 3 specifics.
Status flags: FORWARD-ACTIVE = enforce ongoing through Stage 3 close (or specific item). SATISFIED = closed at named item; remains audit history but no enforcement. SATISFIED-AUDIT-HISTORY = SATISFIED with explicit audit-history retention for Stage 3 narrative. FORWARD-ACTIVE-FIRST-ACTION = priority CRITICAL; Stage 3 EA executes as first substantive action.

ack_group.label: dn15 -- Docs-Native handoff cadence; namespace-collision with do_not_list prefix

This shares a prefix with GUIDE_HANDOFF_LATEST.xml `do_not_list` prohibitions (dn1-dn24) but represents a different namespace: dn15 here is a positive ack ("I will follow this discipline"), not a negative prohibition ("do not do X"). The collision is an authoring convenience, not a functional overlap. EA-6 init pointer missed dn15 entirely.

Stage 3 EA verifies post-3.6 via: Select-String -Path C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\.vscode\mcp.json -Pattern 'VIKUNJA_PASS.*[A-Za-z0-9]{16,}' Expected: NO match (only `${env:VIKUNJA_PASS}` reference should appear). ON ANY MATCH: STOP. Refactor to env-var reference; do NOT commit a literal password into the devplatform copy.

Path-confusion vigilance at Stage 3.1 copy: Stage 3.1 explicitly copies BOTH chokepoint and legacy Vikunja client files: - tools/_vikunja_client.py (NEW chokepoint; created at Stage 2.1; Stage 3 copies to devplatform/tools/_vikunja_client.py) - tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py (LEGACY HTTP client; modified at Stage 2.6 D2-completion + D3; Stage 3 copies via the recursive directory copy of tools/fleet_observability/ to devplatform/tools/fleet_observability/) Both files MUST be copied verbatim. Per dn18 + g5-6, cite full paths consistently in any Stage 3 narrative or commit message; do NOT abbreviate to "_vikunja_client.py" or "the chokepoint" without full path qualification when discussing either file.
Stage 3 EA verifies post-3.1 via: Test-Path C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\tools\_vikunja_client.py Test-Path C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\tools\fleet_observability\_vikunja_client.py Both expected: True. Byte-identity verification optional but recommended (Get-FileHash on each pair).

v2-baseline file copy verification at Stage 3.1.v2: the recursive directory copy at Stage 3.1 covers the v2-baseline files (per Stage 3.1.v2 verify_all_present block), but explicit verification is required to catch any silent omission. ea_initiation.work_plan_pointer:
The Stage 3 XML is the authoritative work plan. Execution order:
Item 3.1 (FIRST after iter-1 APPROVAL): Copy 7 platform tool directories + 2 chokepoint helpers from BlarAI/tools/ to devplatform/tools/. Use Copy-Item -Recurse -Force. Do-not-copy clauses: tools/openvino_contrib_agent/ (BlarAI-only); BlarAI-specific tests (Stage 3 EA reads tools/tests/ to disambiguate platform vs BlarAI tests).
Item 3.1.v2 (verify v2-baseline files present per g7-ea1_n3): Test-Path each of the 6 v2-baseline files at devplatform (blarai_next_task_resolver.py at autonomy_budget/, diff_builder.py at fleet_ops/, escalation_notify.ps1 at fleet_observability/, escalation-watchdog.{xml,ps1} at scheduled-tasks/, test_async_post_gate.ps1 at scheduled-tasks/). All expected: True.
Item 3.2: Copy platform-doctrine docs by pattern (CLAUDE_*.md, CLAUDE_*.xml, CO_LEAD_*.xml, DEC*.xml, DOMAIN*.xml, CONTINUATION_PROMPT.md). Source: BlarAI/docs/. Destination: devplatform/docs/.
Item 3.2.v2 (per g7-ea1_n4): Copy docs/governance/ (recursive; 7 files) + docs/scheduled/wake_templates/ (recursive; 6 files) to devplatform/docs/governance/ + devplatform/docs/scheduled/wake_templates/.
Item 3.3: Copy claude_cowork/ + claude_projects/ if populated (`Get-ChildItem`.Count > 0).
Item 3.4: Populate devplatform/pyproject.toml from BlarAI/pyproject.toml. Filter to platform deps only (yaml, requests/httpx, pytest, etc.); exclude BlarAI-runtime deps (openvino, transformers, etc.). Preserve [tool.pytest.ini_options] testpaths = tools/tests/.
Item 3.5: cd devplatform; .\.venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1; pip install -e . Verify via `python -c "import yaml, requests; print('OK')"` and `pip list | Select-String "devplatform"`.
Item 3.6 (per g7-ea1_n1): Copy .mcp.json + .vscode/mcp.json templates from BlarAI to devplatform; rewrite paths to point to devplatform; replace VIKUNJA_PASS literal with `${env:VIKUNJA_PASS}` env-var reference. Verify NO literal in either devplatform copy via Select-String pattern check.
Item 3.7 (per g7-ea1_n6): Smoke test via: cd C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform; .\.venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1 python -m tools.fleet_observability.daily_digest --project-root C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI --project-id $BLARAI_PID --dry-run Where $BLARAI_PID resolved from BlarAI/.platform/vikunja_project_ids.yaml via the Stage 2.8 / Stage 4.7 preflight regex pattern. Expected: completion without error; output equivalent to BlarAI invocation (subset acceptable since dry-run).
Item 3.8 (per g7-ea1_n7): BlarAI-only docs absence verify in devplatform/docs/ via Get-ChildItem | Select-String -Pattern "Task4|P5_|ADR|LEDGER|IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN|TEST_GOVERNANCE". Expected: empty.
Item 3.9 (per g7-ea1_n8): Single Stage-3 commit on devplatform main via: cd C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform; git add .; git commit -m "<commit_template>" Commit template per g7-ea1_n8 verbatim. Verify post-commit: git log --oneline -1 shows Stage 3 commit; git show --stat shows expected file count (~7 dirs + 2 helpers + N doctrine + governance + wake_templates + pyproject + .venv changes via pip + 2 MCP configs + 1 manifest = ~tens of files); git status --porcelain empty.
Item 3.10 (per g7-ea1_n9): Append-only STATUS.md update on BlarAI side. Append Stage 3 closure entry: Stage Ledger row Stage 3 PENDING -> COMPLETE; Current State table updates; any A12+ anomalies; g7-ea1 ack closure summaries; F-1 mitigation forward-progress; g6-ea6_n8 STATUS-staleness reminder; optional Stage 6.7.5 ticket suggestion. Discipline: A1-A11 + Stage Ledger Stages 0/1/2 rows + Stage 2 Closure Footnotes section + Recovery Anchors + Vikunja Project IDs + How to Resume + prior Execution Log entries ALL UNTOUCHED.
Item 3.10.v2 (per g7-ea1_n5): Write devplatform/.copy_manifest_v2.yaml with v2_files_copied list (LOC totals from BlarAI source). Include in Item 3.9 commit.
Closing handoff to LA: emit per `closing_handoff_to_la_at_stage_3_close` block at the bottom of this init pointer.

stop_conditions.condition: If V0a-V0M pickup verification surfaces drift beyond the expected (working tree clean post-LA-handoff; HEAD 0d61581; fleet paused with original timestamp; stash byte-identical; devplatform main @ eaa005f), STOP. Report which V0 check failed with actual vs expected to LA / Guide-#7 before any work.

If pytest baseline regresses below 351 BEFORE Stage 3 work begins (i.e., V0g fails), STOP. Stage 2 closure should have left a clean 351 baseline; regression indicates external interference. Investigate before proceeding.

If Stage 3.1 Copy-Item -Recurse fails or produces a partial tree (e.g., access denied; lock contention; missing source file), STOP. Re-run with -Force only after diagnosing the failure mode. Do NOT silently overwrite if any file in destination differs from expected (would mask state corruption).

If Stage 3.1.v2 verify_all_present block returns False for any v2-baseline file, STOP. Re-run targeted Copy-Item for the missing file with byte-hash verification. Document disposition in 3.10 STATUS append.

If Stage 3.5 `pip install -e .` fails (e.g., dep resolution conflict, missing Python version, network access denied for any package), STOP. Diagnose pyproject.toml content first; do NOT modify dep list silently. Engage LA for any external intervention requirement.

If Stage 3.6 MCP config rewriting produces a literal VIKUNJA_PASS in either devplatform copy (per g7-ea1_n1 Select-String pattern check), STOP. Re-edit to use `${env:VIKUNJA_PASS}` reference; do NOT commit a config containing a plaintext literal.

If Stage 3.7 smoke test --dry-run produces error output OR writes any state (state.json modification, vikunja DB write, bridge inbox/processed mutation), STOP. The --dry-run flag is REQUIRED to prevent state writes; if it failed, investigate tools/fleet_observability/daily_digest.py implementation for --dry-run handling regression. NO silent state correction.
If Stage 3.7 $BLARAI_PID preflight returns empty or any value other than 3, STOP. Per g4-5: NO silent regex/yaml adaptation. Report to LA.

If Stage 3.8 BlarAI-only docs absence Select-String returns non-empty (any Task4*, P5_*, ADR*, LEDGER*, IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN*, TEST_GOVERNANCE* match in devplatform/docs/), STOP. Investigate which Stage 3.2 pattern overshot; remove unintended copy from devplatform; preserve BlarAI original; document disposition.

If Stage 3.9 commit produces more than ONE commit on devplatform (e.g., accidentally split across multiple invocations) OR omits any expected file (manifest, MCP configs, doctrine docs, pyproject.toml), STOP and investigate. NO partial commits; NO emergency single-commit attempts that omit work.

If Stage 3.10 STATUS.md append modifies any pre-existing section (A1-A11, Stage Ledger Stages 0/1/2, Stage 2 Closure Footnotes, Recovery Anchors, Vikunja Project IDs, How to Resume, prior Execution Log entries), STOP and revert. Append-only discipline strictly preserved per g3-3 + g7-ea1_n9.

If context exhaustion threatens at any clean breakpoint before Item 3.9 (post-3.5 dep-install close; post-3.7 smoke-test close; post-3.8 docs-absence-verify close), emit Stage-3-mid-stage-handoff XML following v1-v5 schema (docs-only commit; ONE file in diff; commit message references the breakpoint reached). NO partial-commit refactor. NO emergency single-commit attempts. Per i1-4 + g6-ea6_n4.

If you discover any procedural defect in Stage 3 XML (e.g., a path drift like R2 wake_launcher; a regex defect; a verify command typo), STOP and report to LA + Guide-#7. Stage XMLs are locked artifacts; defect reports go in EA-1 working notes for potential post-procedure review (NOT inline-fix without explicit LA authorization).
